pages.raw_text:
The Northern Pacific railroad has


established a sort of crematory for


old ties and waste lumber at the


Mill st. crossing. Danger of loss
is thereby inflicted on the neighbor-


hood, as the fires are started with
out regard to proximity to build
ings, apparently, and no one left to
watch them. A big fire was burn
ing there the other night within
less than 100 feet of building, and
there was no railroad employee in


sight. Tho policy of the section
men seems to be to start the fires


and let the neighborhood take
care Of the houses or be burned
out.


City ordinances provide against
danger from such sources, but 1t
has been so long since the North
ern Pacific attempted to obey a
city ordinance that the heads of
departments have probably forgot
ten that such things exist. The or
dinances are not automatic, unfor-
tunately.
